title: Upgrade the Action
learningGoal: The learner will successfully upgrade base subject-verb verb clauses into Present Perfect declarative statements by accurately deploying the 'Have/Has' auxiliary and converting the base verb to its V3 participle form.
instructions: A beginner student wrote three very boring, simple sentences about what is happening right now: 'I wash the car.' 'He eats the apple.' 'They go home.' Your job is to UPGRADE them! Turn them into the Present Perfect tense to show the tasks are 'ALREADY FINISHED'.

contentItems.text: CRITICAL RULE: Remember to choose Have or Has carefully! Then, you must change the verb to Verb 3 (washed, eaten, gone).

writingPrompt: Change these 3 sentences into Present Perfect: 1. I wash the car. 2. He eats the apple. 3. They go home.
promptGoal: Test independent morphological conversion of base verbs to V3 participles and the correct alignment of Have/Has auxiliaries with subject pronouns.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: I have washed the car. He has eaten the apple. They have gone home.
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: आई हैव वॉशड द कार। ही हैज़ ईटन द ऐपल। दे हैव गॉन होम।
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: मैंने कार धो ली है। उसने सेब खा लिया है। वे घर जा चुके हैं।
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect score. The learner successfully matched the subjects to their respective auxiliaries (I have, He has, They have) and generated the V3 forms (washed, eaten, gone).

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: I have wash the car. He have eat the apple. They has go home.
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: आई हैव वॉश द कार। ही हैव ईट द ऐपल। दे हैज़ गो होम।
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Critical failure. Massive morphological errors. Failure to use V3 ('have wash'), failure to match 3rd person singular ('He have'), and auxiliary mismatch on plural ('They has').

evaluationCriteria.description: Did the user definitively choose 'have' for I/They, and 'has' for He?
Did the user successfully modify the base verbs to washed, eaten, and gone? (Heavy penalization for leaving them in base/V1 form).

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: I have washed the car. He has eaten the apple. They have gone home.
acceptableAlternates: I've washed the car. He's eaten the apple. They've gone home.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Absolute zero tolerance for base verb retention. 'I have wash' instantly fails over 50% of the rubric.

feedbackRules.onPass: Magnificent! You successfully upgraded the sentences! You perfectly matched 'He' with 'Has', and you proved you know the V3 irregular verbs (Eaten, Gone)!
feedbackRules.onFail: Please review your writing. Two rules are critical: 1. 'He/She/It' gets HAS. The others get HAVE. 2. You MUST use Verb 3. You cannot say 'He has eat'. You must say 'He has eaten'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good start! Double check Sentence 2 and Sentence 3. 'Eat' turns into 'Eaten'. 'Go' turns into 'Gone'!

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Change the sentences to Present Perfect. I wash the car. He eats the apple. They go home.

hintText: Match the pronoun: I [have], He [has], They [have]. Then change the verbs: wash->washed, eat->eaten, go->gone.
commonMistakes: Writing 'He have eaten' (L1 speakers often generalize 'have' across all pronouns).
Writing 'They have went' (mixing Simple Past V2 with Past Participle V3).
